commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7397fd594a86b1e6c16d7b8dba8b9e7c5beed5a0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a QQBot `MediaApi.uploadMedia()` direct-upload URL preflight using the existing SSRF policy and adds focused Vitest coverage for accepted, non-HTTPS, and policy-rejected URLs.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +123. Total +143 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main forwards `opts.url` directly into the QQ upload body, and the PR’s mocked tests show rejected URLs failing before token lookup or API calls. I did not run a live QQ upload.
commands.review_followup: The PR has a protected `maintainer` label and the remaining blocker is a security/compatibility policy decision, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(qqbot): validate direct media upload urls
target.body:
## Summary

Validate QQBot direct media upload URLs before they are forwarded to the QQ media upload API.

## Changes

- Reintroduced direct-upload URL preflight at the `MediaApi.uploadMedia()` sink.
- Requires direct-upload URLs to use HTTPS and pass the existing `QQBOT_MEDIA_SSRF_POLICY` via `resolvePinnedHostnameWithPolicy()`.
- Added regression coverage for allowed image/video/file URL uploads, non-HTTPS rejection, and SSRF-policy rejection before token lookup or QQ API calls.

## Validation

Behavior addressed: QQBot direct media URL uploads are validated before the QQ API request body is built.

Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout with repo dependencies hydrated via `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm format -- extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm format:check -- extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Evidence after fix: the focused Vitest target passed with 1 file and 5 tests; formatting and diff whitespace checks passed; autoreview returned no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: non-HTTPS and SSRF-policy-rejected direct-upload URLs fail before token lookup or `client.request()`, while allowed QQ/Tencent media URLs proceed to the upload request body.

What was not tested: live QQ API upload with real credentials; broad `check:changed` or full-suite validation.

## Notes

- Security-sensitive exploit details are intentionally omitted from the public PR body.
- AI-assisted.
- `CHANGELOG.md` was not updated.
- No agent transcript is included.
target.author: eleqtrizit
